text.title: Ollama Production Deployment — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-deployment methodology. Sources: Ollama documentation — server / serve flags; NVIDIA CUDA + container-toolkit docs; Nginx reverse proxy guidance for streaming LLM APIs.

rule.statement: Pin GPU allocation explicitly via OLLAMA_GPU_LAYERS / OLLAMA_NUM_GPU and never share a single device across two Ollama daemons without explicit isolation.
rule.rationale: Unpinned GPU allocation causes VRAM thrash and silent fallback to CPU inference, masking 10–100× latency regressions.

rule.statement: Bind Ollama to 127.0.0.1 by default; only expose 0.0.0.0 behind a reverse proxy that enforces auth, TLS, and rate-limiting.
rule.rationale: An open Ollama endpoint lets anyone enqueue inference jobs, exhausting GPU and burning runtime budget.

rule.statement: Preload required models on service start (OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=-1 or systemd ExecStartPost: `ollama pull <model>`) so the first user request is not a 30-second cold start.

rationale.source: Nginx reverse proxy guidance for streaming LLM APIs

rule.rationale: Cold-start latency dominates p99 for low-traffic deployments and produces unpredictable SLO violations.

rule.statement: Set systemd resource limits (MemoryMax, CPUQuota) and Linux cgroup VRAM caps; document the model→memory footprint table in the runbook.
rule.rationale: Without limits a runaway query OOM-kills the host; the footprint table is the only reproducible way to size a new model.

rule.statement: Export Prometheus textfile metrics (queue depth, model load events, tokens/s, GPU utilisation) and wire a Grafana dashboard before going live.
rule.rationale: Without metrics, capacity planning is folklore and the first production incident has no signal to diagnose.
